primary_variables.matched_definition: Intervention using a compound that inhibits a target protein, pathway, or process.
evidence: the in vivo treatment with ATRA/VP also has direct effects on the fundamental functions of human AML cells
VP is a branched short-chain fatty acid that targets two of the four classes of HDACs
the combination of RARA/RXRA agonists and HDAC antagonists should be further explored

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between disease and matched control cohorts.
evidence: compare the primary AML cells of responders and non-responders to ATRA/VP
We studied the proteome profiles of patient cells derived from 11 responders (PRE-R samples) and 17 non-responders (PRE-NR samples).

attributes.agent: all-trans retinoic acid (ATRA) and valproic acid (VP)
attributes.dose_or_concentration: ATRA (22.5 mg/m2 orally twice daily); VP target serum level being 300–600 μmol/L
evidence: combined therapy with ATRA (22.5 mg/m2 orally twice daily for 14 days every 12th week), continuous oral VP treatment

LC–MS/MS raw files from 11 responder and 17 non-responder samples from both studies before treatment at day 1 (i.e., PRE-R and PRE-NR), and from 5 responder and 5 non-responder samples (from the ATRA–VP–TP study only) at day 1, day 3 and day 8

replicate_hints: 11 responder and 17 non-responder samples from both studies before treatment at day 1
multiplex_channel_hints: super-SILAC (stable isotope labeling by amino acids in cell culture) mix

evidence: We found 98 differentially expressed proteins, where 55 proteins were upregulated and 43 were downregulated in the PRE-R relative to the PRE-NR group
investigate the effects of in vivo treatment with ATRA/VP on primary AML cells
paired t-tests were performed to find significant differences in the PRE vs. 3D, PRE vs. 8D and 3D vs. 8D comparisons
